steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
Use two points from the table, $(1,46)$ and $(2,43)$. The change in $g(x)$ is $-3$ when $x$ increases by $1$, so the slope is $-3$.
$$m=\frac{43-46}{2-1}=-3$$

steps.title: Substitute a table point
steps.content: Use $g(x)=mx+b$ with $m=-3$ and the point $(1,46)$. Substituting the point gives an equation for the constant term. $$46=-3(1)+b$$ $$46=-3+b$$ $$b=49$$ This is the requested value of the constant $b$. The answer is 49.
